novel_features.name: Quick expense from natural language
novel_features.description: File an expense with one line: amount, merchant, and category parsed from a short prompt — no forms, no web UI.
novel_features.rationale: Local parser extracts amount/merchant/category/date before hitting the API, so agents can one-shot file expenses without interactive forms the Expensify web app demands.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When a user tells an agent 'I just expensed dinner at Maya for $42.50,' the agent should file it in one call — not walk through six fields.

novel_features.name: Draft report from date range
novel_features.description: Create a report and auto-attach every un-reported expense from a date range in a single command.
novel_features.rationale: Requires local expense cache + a single transactional attach — web UI requires clicking each expense one at a time.
novel_features.why_it_matters: End-of-month submission turns from 45 clicks into one command.

novel_features.name: What's the damage
novel_features.description: Single-glance summary: total expensed, pending, approved, paid for the current month (or a custom range).
novel_features.rationale: Local rollup over cached reports + expenses; web dashboard scatters these numbers across three screens.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents asked 'how much did I expense this month' get one answer in one call.

novel_features.name: Receipt folder watcher
novel_features.description: Daemon mode — drop a receipt PNG/JPG/PDF into a folder, it auto-files as an expense.
novel_features.rationale: Runs locally and uses OCR + category suggestion heuristics trained on your own expense history. No web tool does this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: A scanner app or Siri shortcut drops files into Dropbox; this files them automatically.

novel_features.name: Expense from bank line
novel_features.description: Paste a raw bank or card CSV row and the CLI extracts date/merchant/amount/currency and files the expense.
novel_features.rationale: Local regex + heuristics; no API has a 'parse this bank line' endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reconciling AmEx? Paste a row, file an expense. No copy-paste-copy-paste.

novel_features.name: Offline expense search
novel_features.description: FTS5 search over all your expenses by merchant, comment, category, or tag. Regex-friendly.
novel_features.rationale: Local SQLite store indexed with FTS5; Expensify's API has only exact-match filtering.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents asked 'did I expense that Starbucks last month' get an answer in one local query.

novel_features.name: Missing receipts
novel_features.description: Lists expenses without attached receipts so you can catch them before submitting a report.
novel_features.rationale: Local query across the expense store; web UI only surfaces this per-report, not globally.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Submit-report-and-get-bounced feels bad; surface missing receipts upfront.

novel_features.name: Monthly rollup
novel_features.description: Pivot-table expenses by category, tag, or merchant for any time range.
novel_features.rationale: Local SQL aggregate; Expensify web has report-level totals but not cross-report rollups.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Build your own spending dashboard without burning API budget.

novel_features.name: Duplicate detection
novel_features.description: Finds expenses that look like duplicates by (merchant, amount, date±window).
novel_features.rationale: Local fuzzy match across the expense store; nothing similar in the API or web UI.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Accidental double-file is a top AP pain point; surface it before submission.

novel_features.name: Submit and wait
novel_features.description: Blocks until the report transitions past SUBMITTED, great for CI pipelines.
novel_features.rationale: Local polling with backoff; the API returns immediately.
novel_features.why_it_matters: CI jobs that submit expense reports can wait for approval before moving on.

novel_features.name: Undo last action
novel_features.description: Revert the last create/edit/submit action from the local action log.
novel_features.rationale: Local append-only log tracks every mutation; web UI has no undo.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents sometimes file the wrong amount; one command to roll it back.

novel_features.name: MCP bridge
novel_features.description: Expose every subcommand as an MCP tool so Claude Desktop and other MCP clients can drive Expensify.
novel_features.rationale: Leverages the whole CLI surface; no one has shipped a Go MCP for Expensify (the two existing ones are TS/JS and cover < half of the command set).
novel_features.why_it_matters: Claude Desktop gets a single-install Expensify integration that matches this CLI's coverage exactly.

novel_features.name: Bulk close
novel_features.description: End-of-month orchestrator: list reports in range, export with GL template, download the file, mark-as-exported atomically.
novel_features.rationale: Combines multiple Integration Server jobs with 429 backoff + idempotent label sentinel. Web UI has no 'close month' button.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Finance teams close a month in one command instead of a 40-click sequence.

novel_features.name: Policy diff
novel_features.description: Compare local YAML policy config against live API to preview changes before applying.
novel_features.rationale: Local YAML <-> API diff for categories/tags/rules. No Expensify tool does this.
novel_features.example: expensify-pp-cli admin policy diff POLICY_ID_HERE categories.yaml
novel_features.why_it_matters: Manage chart-of-accounts as code with dry-run safety.

novel_features.name: Smart category suggest
novel_features.description: When filing an expense, suggests a category based on prior classifications for the same merchant.
novel_features.rationale: Local history lookup ('you last categorized Uber as Transportation'); no API has this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Filing is faster when the category is already right 90% of the time.

narrative.headline: File expenses and submit reports to Expensify in one line. Every command an agent should need, with a local cache so searches stay offline.
narrative.value_prop: expensify-pp-cli turns the Expensify web app into a terminal. Log in once, and every filing/reviewing/submitting task that used to require clicking through forms becomes a single command. A local SQLite store gives you offline search, rollups, dupe detection, and missing-receipt alerts that no other Expensify tool has.
narrative.auth_narrative: Two ways to authenticate: (1) `expensify auth login` opens a browser, you log in, the CLI captures your session token — works immediately for all filing/submitting commands; (2) `expensify auth set-keys` stores your Integration Server partner credentials (get them at https://www.expensify.com/tools/integrations/) — required only for export/admin commands. Most users only need option 1.

quickstart.comment: Browser-based login — captures your Expensify session
Pull your workspaces, expenses, and reports into a local cache
File your first expense in one line
Create a report and attach every un-reported expense from April
Submit for approval and block until it moves forward

troubleshoots.fix: Your session expired. Run `expensify-pp-cli auth login` again.
Integration Server enforces 5 req / 10s and 20 req / 60s. The CLI backs off automatically; if you see this, wait 60 seconds before retrying.
Export commands use the Integration Server, not your session. Run `expensify-pp-cli auth set-keys` with credentials from https://www.expensify.com/tools/integrations/.
Format: `<description> <merchant> $<amount>`. Example: `"Dinner at Maya $42.50"`. Use `--amount/--merchant/--category` flags for explicit control.
Run `expensify-pp-cli sync` to refresh the local cache.

narrative.when_to_use: Pick this CLI when you want to file, review, or submit Expensify expenses and reports without leaving the terminal. It excels at one-liner expense filing from natural language, end-of-month report drafting from a date range, offline search across years of expenses, and orchestrating accounting-system exports. Agents can drive it through the standard --json output and typed exit codes, or through the built-in MCP bridge mode.

recipes.title: File a quick expense
recipes.explanation: One line, one call, one expense. Category auto-suggested from your history.

recipes.title: Draft this month's report
recipes.explanation: Creates a report and attaches every un-reported April expense in a single command.

recipes.title: Submit for approval and wait
recipes.explanation: Blocks until approval arrives — drop this in CI after a closing script.

recipes.title: Find expenses with no receipt
recipes.explanation: Catches receipt gaps before they bounce your report.

recipes.title: Close the month (admin)
recipes.explanation: End-to-end: list approved reports, export with the NetSuite template, download the file, mark as exported.

trigger_phrases: what did I expense this month
